questions.question_text: Describe your firm's investment strategy and philosophy.
questions.answer_text: Meridian Value Partners employs a concentrated deep value approach focused on U.S. small-cap equities. Invests in high-quality businesses temporarily mispriced due to short-term market dislocations, management transitions, or sector-wide sell-offs. Typically holds 15-20 positions with a 2-4 year investment horizon.

questions.question_text: What is your target market segment, geography, and sector focus?
questions.answer_text: U.S. small-cap value equities, $200M - $5B market cap. Primarily domestic. Opportunistic across sectors.

questions.question_text: Describe your investment process from sourcing to exit.
questions.answer_text: Sourcing through quantitative screens and industry relationships. Deep fundamental analysis with emphasis on normalized earnings power. Investment committee approval required (majority vote).

questions.question_text: What is your target fund size and investment period?
questions.answer_text: Meridian Concentrated Value Fund II target: $1.2 billion. Hard cap: $1.5 billion. Current AUM: $1.8 billion.

questions.question_text: Describe your portfolio construction approach, including diversification targets.
questions.answer_text: 15-20 positions. Maximum single position: 10% at cost. No explicit sector limits. Cash range: 0-15%.

questions.question_text: Describe your investment committee process and approval requirements.
questions.answer_text: Three-person investment committee: James Liu (CIO), Michael Torres (PM), and one rotating senior analyst. Majority vote required. James Liu has tie-breaking authority.

28 employees total. Investment Team (12): James Liu (Founder, Managing Partner & CIO, founded 2015, 20 yrs experience), Michael Torres (Partner, PM, joined 2016, 15 yrs experience), 4 Senior Analysts, 4 Analysts, 2 Research Associates. Operations (10): COO, CCO, CFO, 7 operations staff. Client Service (4): Head of IR, 3 staff. Technology (2): CTO, 1 developer.

questions.question_text: How many total employees does the firm have?
questions.answer_text: 28 total employees as of September 30, 2025.
James Liu: 72%, Michael Torres: 18%, Employee pool: 10%.
Sole key person: James Liu. Key person event triggers fund suspension.

questions.question_text: Have any key investment professionals departed in the last 3 years?
questions.answer_text: Yes. Dr. Rebecca Santos, former Head of Research and Partner (15% ownership), departed in March 2025 to join a competitor. Her departure reduced the senior investment team from 4 to 3 partners. Her ownership stake was redistributed to James Liu (7%) and the employee pool (8%).
GP commitment: $18 million (1.5% of target). James Liu: $15M, Michael Torres: $3M.
Michael Torres is the designated successor CIO. Formal succession plan is being updated following Dr. Santos's departure. Expected completion: Q2 2026.
$1.8 billion as of September 30, 2025.

questions.question_text: Provide gross and net returns for each fund since inception.
questions.answer_text: Value Fund I (2015): 13.7% gross / 11.2% net vs 10.1% benchmark. Value Fund II (2020): 15.3% gross / 12.8% net vs 9.8% benchmark.

questions.question_text: Provide annualized returns net of all fees.
questions.answer_text: Fund II net returns: 1yr 11.5% (bench 8.7%, +2.8%), 3yr 10.9% (bench 7.9%, +3.0%), since inception 12.8% (bench 9.8%, +3.0%).
Q1 2020 COVID: -31.2% vs -27.3% benchmark (underperformed by 3.9%). 2022 rate shock: -16.8% vs -14.5% benchmark (underperformed by 2.3%).

questions.question_text: Has the track record been audited?
questions.answer_text: Yes. Audited by KPMG LLP.
Yes. GIPS-compliant since 2018.
Management fee: 100 basis points on NAV.

questions.question_text: Carried interest rate and preferred return?
questions.answer_text: Performance fee: 20% above a 5% hurdle rate, high-water mark.
Risk management is overseen by the CIO with support from the COO. Position limits and sector guidelines are monitored daily. No formal risk committee.
SEC-registered investment adviser. CRD Number: 301892.

questions.question_text: Any regulatory investigations or enforcement actions?
questions.answer_text: No enforcement actions. One routine SEC examination in 2023 with no findings.
No. The firm is evaluating PRI signatory status for 2026.
Sustainability considerations are reviewed informally during the due diligence process. No formal sustainability policy document.
SOC 2 Type I certified. Annual penetration testing. MFA required for all systems.

gaps.question_text: What is your typical deal size range?
Describe your sourcing process and competitive advantages in deal origination.
What is your due diligence process for evaluating potential investments?
What is your approach to value creation post-investment?
Describe your exit strategy and typical holding period.

Has there been any change in ownership in the last 5 years?
List all other funds or accounts managed.
Quarterly return history for most recent 5 years?
Total investments and realized vs unrealized split?
Does the management fee step down?
List all fees and expenses borne by the fund.
Any material risk limit breaches in past 5 years?
Code of ethics and personal trading?
Amended ADV disclosures for disciplinary events?

flags.description: Key person risk: sole key person is James Liu (72% ownership). Dr. Rebecca Santos, former Head of Research and Partner, departed in March 2025 to join a competitor, reducing the senior investment team from 4 to 3 partners. Fund suspension triggered by a single individual's departure.

Risk management section is critically sparse: only 1 question answered out of 12+. No formal risk committee, no independent risk function, no stress testing described, no breach history disclosed. Risk oversight concentrated in the CIO (who is also the sole key person).

Incomplete DDQ: only 22 of 150+ ILPA questions answered (14.7% completeness). Multiple entire sections are sparse or missing, including fees (2 answers), compliance (2 answers), risk management (1 answer), and cybersecurity (1 answer).

Downside underperformance in both major drawdowns: Q1 2020 -31.2% vs -27.3% benchmark (3.9% worse), 2022 -16.8% vs -14.5% benchmark (2.3% worse). Concentrated deep value strategy amplifies losses in downturns, contradicting the expectation of downside protection from value discipline.
Succession plan is not finalized. Currently 'being updated' following Dr. Santos's departure, with expected completion Q2 2026. No formal plan in place for a firm with a sole key person.
No formal sustainability policy and not a PRI signatory. Sustainability considerations reviewed only informally. May not meet LP responsible investing requirements.
SOC 2 Type I only (not Type II). Type I certifies controls at a point in time; Type II certifies operating effectiveness over a period. Less assurance than industry best practice.

follow_up_questions.question: With James Liu as sole key person and Dr. Santos's recent departure, what interim safeguards are in place if James Liu were unable to serve? What is the timeline and scope of the updated succession plan?

The risk management section is extremely sparse. Can you provide details on: (a) risk limits and guidelines, (b) stress testing methodology, (c) liquidity risk management, and (d) any plans to establish a formal risk committee?
The fund underperformed its benchmark in both major drawdowns (Q1 2020 and 2022). What risk controls or portfolio adjustments have been implemented to improve downside capture?
How has the investment process and research coverage been affected by Dr. Santos's departure? Have any additional senior hires been made or planned to replace her?
Please describe the compliance program, including the CCO's background, reporting structure, and testing procedures.
What is the distribution waterfall for Fund II?
Can you provide quarterly return history for Fund II since inception?
What are the fund term, extension provisions, and liquidity terms for Fund II?
Please describe the valuation methodology and frequency for unrealized positions.
What insurance coverage does the firm maintain (E&O, D&O, cyber, fidelity)?
What is the total expense ratio including all fees borne by the fund?
Is there any pending or threatened litigation against the firm or its principals?
Does the management fee step down at higher commitment levels?
What is the firm's timeline for achieving PRI signatory status and developing a formal sustainability policy?
Are there plans to upgrade from SOC 2 Type I to Type II certification?
